reasoning: Strong fundamentals with declining ROE is the classic bank value trap setup. The 57% gain provides cushion, but the stock is expensive relative to analyst targets. The geopolitical risk (Iran war, cyber threats to banks) adds uncertainty. Hold — the dividend is solid and the bank is well-capitalized, but no reason to add here. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Add on a pullback to 20,000 ILA (near 200-day MA); reduce if ROE drops below 15%.
entryConditions: Add on a pullback to 20,000 ILA (near 200-day MA)
bullCase: Mizrahi Tefahot is Israel's third-largest bank with strong FY2025 results (NIS 5.63B net profit, +3.2% YoY). Loans +12%, deposits +14% show balance sheet expansion. P/E 10.3x is fair for a bank with declining ROE. Tier 1 capital at 10.24% is solid. Successfully raised USD 1.45B internationally.
bearCase: ROE declining for 3 consecutive years (20.1%→18.5%→17%) due to rate compression. Bank of Israel NIS 6.8M AML fine (Mar 2026) is minor but negative optics. RSI deeply oversold at 21 — stock down 14% from ATH. Analyst target at 21,600 ILA is below current price. Israel-Iran war constrains BoI rate cuts.
